title: The Wettest Stories Ever Told

jokes.text: See, it goes ship's wheel, popeye tattoo, gilligan hat, fish with boobs and back to ship's wheel.
jokes.setup: Character claims to have figured out wallpaper pattern from boredom
jokes.punchline: Pattern includes absurd maritime elements culminating in 'fish with boobs'
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with unexpected escalation to absurd imagery. Good economy and specificity.

jokes.text: What about this swordfish? My life's work ruined!
jokes.setup: Bart claims to have decoded the wallpaper pattern completely
jokes.punchline: Immediately discovers another element that destroys his 'analysis'
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation with excellent economy. Classic Simpsons structure of immediate consequence.

jokes.text: Red lobster? Not that good.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain offers to get food from a better restaurant
jokes.punchline: Red Lobster is dismissed as still not good enough
jokes.explanation: Mild restaurant humor playing on chain restaurant stereotypes. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Yes, the ship that brought prostitutes to America.
jokes.setup: Reference to the Mayflower's historical importance
jokes.punchline: Homer confidently misidentifies Protestants as prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - confidently wrong with sexual confusion. Great character integration and quotable malaprism.

jokes.text: I've got my toy wood lump. What jolly fun.
jokes.setup: Asked about shipboard entertainment
jokes.punchline: Puritan child's toy is literally just a lump of wood, delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Good historical satire about Puritan austerity. The deadpan 'what jolly fun' sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: Out of my way, you god-fearing buckleheads!
jokes.setup: Wanted man fleeing through Puritan crowd
jokes.punchline: Creative period-appropriate insult 'buckleheads' referencing Puritan hat buckles
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that feels both period-appropriate and naturally insulting. Good creative writing.

jokes.text: He's wanted for daring to question why we call this the jacobean era when the king's name is James and not Jacob.
jokes.setup: Soldiers searching for dangerous criminal
jokes.punchline: Crime is pedantic historical linguistics question
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated meta-joke about historical naming conventions. Very Simpsons - taking academic pedantry to absurd extremes.

jokes.text: Oh, lord, please let the soldiers kill this family instead of me.
jokes.setup: Family thinks he's praying devoutly when cornered
jokes.punchline: Prayer is actually selfish plea to sacrifice his protectors
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer selfishness disguised as piety. Dark but perfectly character-driven. Excellent misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Then the codpiece holds no terrors for thee.
jokes.setup: Learning Marge is a widow
jokes.punchline: Immediately makes crude sexual advance using period terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer sexual opportunism with period-appropriate language. Character-driven but predictable.

jokes.text: What kind of a booze cruise is this? Where's the hooch?
jokes.setup: Barney among the Puritans on the Mayflower
jokes.punchline: Expects alcohol on the most abstemious religious voyage in history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney moment - his alcoholism clashing with Puritan values. Good character integration.

jokes.text: We puritans have no place for drunkenness... or colorful clothes or dreaming or poetry. So if you write a sonnet, keep it under your bonnet.
jokes.setup: Listing things Puritans forbid
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates rhyming poetry while forbidding poetry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic contradiction - forbids poetry while inadvertently creating it. Perfect setup for Ned's self-horror.

jokes.text: Oh, no! That was a poem! Forgive me, lord!
jokes.setup: Just delivered accidental rhyme while forbidding poetry
jokes.punchline: Horrified self-recognition and immediate repentance
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the previous joke. Ned's extreme religious guilt is perfectly characterized.

jokes.text: Constance Prudence Chastity Goodfaith. My friends call me Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for her name
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long virtue-based Puritan name contrasted with simple 'Marge'
jokes.explanation: Good historical satire of Puritan naming conventions. The contrast with 'Marge' works well.

jokes.text: I didn't kill her husband just so... I mean, I-I didn't kill her husband.
jokes.setup: Claiming engagement to Marge
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals/implies he murdered her husband
jokes.explanation: Classic verbal slip revealing dark secret. Good structure with the fumbled cover-up.

jokes.text: Such language from a woman. Oh, no! I just thought of you as a woman!
jokes.setup: Reacting to woman speaking boldly
jokes.punchline: Realizes even acknowledging her gender is sinful
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Puritan guilt. Ned's extreme religious anxiety is well-characterized.

jokes.text: The sun which bakes our lips to the point of bleeding and your hilarious idea to surround us with water that would kill us if we drank it.
jokes.setup: Giving thanks for God's love
jokes.punchline: Lists objectively terrible conditions as examples of divine love
jokes.explanation: Good dark irony about finding divine purpose in suffering. Captures Puritan mindset well.

jokes.text: Even for this day and age, I'm considered a bad husband.
jokes.setup: Homer courting Marge in 1620
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges being substandard even by historically low standards
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about historical gender dynamics. Classic Homer self-deprecation.

jokes.text: She's going to marry him 'cause he wears boots instead of blackening his feet.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Marge would choose Homer
jokes.punchline: Bizarre historical detail about foot-blackening as primitive footwear
jokes.explanation: Weird historical detail that's probably made up but feels authentic. Odd specificity works.

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure the way to win her heart is to be fat and crying.
jokes.setup: Bart crying about losing Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically describes his own winning qualities
jokes.explanation: Decent self-aware humor. Homer's realistic assessment of his appeal.

jokes.text: Don't stop. You're choking him just the way his father used to. Good times.
jokes.setup: Homer choking Bart in fight
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic reminiscence about childhood abuse delivered cheerfully
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child abuse delivered with inappropriate nostalgia. Disturbing but funny.

jokes.text: Yes, the weather is fair.
jokes.setup: Homer warns about 'exchanging pleasantries' as scandalous
jokes.punchline: Cut to them literally just discussing weather innocently
jokes.explanation: Good comedic juxtaposition showing the innocence of what was presented as scandalous.

jokes.text: Man! That guy sends my humors from sanguine to bilious! That's how we talk. Weird?
jokes.setup: Expressing anger in period-appropriate medical terms
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to acknowledge the weird historical language
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about historical dialogue. The self-awareness works well.

jokes.text: Beer? I thought you guys didn't drink on the gayflower?
jokes.setup: Homer offers beer to Puritans
jokes.punchline: Barney calls the Mayflower the 'gayflower'
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay that fits Barney's character but relies on cheap gay humor.

jokes.text: I was saving this booze to mess up the indians, but seeing what good pals we are, I wanted to give you a taste.
jokes.setup: Homer offering alcohol to Puritans
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions plan to use alcohol to harm Native Americans
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference delivered casually. Acknowledges real colonial harm but risks being insensitive.

jokes.text: Take a sip every time a wave hits the ship. You're good at this.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests drinking game
jokes.punchline: Ned immediately gets drunk due to constant waves
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. The inevitable result of the drinking game on a ship.

jokes.text: Obviously, kissing your ass is getting me nowhere.
jokes.setup: Ned giving religious thanks during storm
jokes.punchline: Drunk Ned suddenly becomes profane and direct with God
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character reversal - pious Ned becoming crude when drunk. Perfect shock value and character work.

jokes.text: Sin, sin, sin! Horseplay? Roughhousing? Horsehousing?
jokes.setup: Discovering crew drinking and playing
jokes.punchline: Escalates from 'roughhousing' to made-up 'horsehousing'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay escalation. The made-up compound word is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: But, baby, a man has needs.
jokes.setup: Marge rejecting Homer after his deception
jokes.punchline: Homer's pathetic plea using cliched pickup line
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer sexual desperation. Character-appropriate but unoriginal.

jokes.text: When we landed I was going to denounce my sister as a witch.
jokes.setup: Lamenting they'll never reach the new world
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of planned witch accusation against sister
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about witch trials. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 31 + 43? 74. Witch, witch, witch!
jokes.setup: Argument about Lisa's mathematical abilities being witchcraft
jokes.punchline: Simple addition problem proves witchcraft accusation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - basic math as evidence of witchcraft. Good historical satire about ignorance.

jokes.text: I've been driving drunk since I was 12.
jokes.setup: Volunteering to save the ship
jokes.punchline: Qualification is extensive drunk driving experience from childhood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - terrible qualification presented as credential. Dark but character-perfect.

jokes.text: I'll see to it you fundamentalist christians live to take over all America by the 21st century.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to save the Puritans
jokes.punchline: Anachronistic reference to modern Christian fundamentalism
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on religious influence in America. Anachronistic but pointed.

jokes.text: Man, that bird did it all. Good guy.
jokes.setup: Albatross leads them to safety
jokes.punchline: Homer's understated appreciation for being saved
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer casual response to miraculous events. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: I just thought of a name for where we're going. New England. That's real creative. What do you call your foot, 'new hand'?
jokes.setup: Bart suggests 'New England' as name
jokes.punchline: Lisa mocks the uncreative naming convention with absurd example
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about colonial naming laziness. The 'new hand' example is perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: Land ho! What did you call me?
jokes.setup: Traditional nautical call of 'Land ho!'
jokes.punchline: Barney mishears it as personal insult
jokes.explanation: Standard wordplay joke. Predictable but economical. Fits Barney's confrontational nature.

jokes.text: I, almost regret what we europeans are going to do to you.
jokes.setup: Thanking Native Americans for help
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions upcoming genocide with mild regret
jokes.explanation: Dark historical commentary delivered with Homer's casual insensitivity. Uncomfortable but pointed.

jokes.text: Also we're going to take your land and wipe you out. Who wants whipped topping?
jokes.setup: Continuing casual mention of genocide
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifts to cheerful dessert offer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tonal whiplash from genocide to dessert. The casual transition is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Well, one time I read this boring comic book about a ship called the bounty. Like the paper towels.
jokes.setup: Bart offering to tell maritime story
jokes.punchline: Reduces HMS Bounty to paper towel brand comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Bart's disinterest in history. The brand association is appropriately juvenile.

jokes.text: Bligh... eats... dolphin... boogers.
jokes.setup: Reading important message from Admiral Nelson
jokes.punchline: Message is juvenile insult about dolphin boogers
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor fitting Bart's character. The buildup makes the childish payoff work.

jokes.text: When you eat as much dolphin as I do there's bound to be a booger or two in the mix.
jokes.setup: Accused of eating dolphin boogers
jokes.punchline: Calmly acknowledges it as statistical inevitability
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that makes the absurd seem logical. Fits Burns' character well.

jokes.text: My father's alive? No, he died while baking. It's all in the letter.
jokes.setup: Milhouse excited about father being alive
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction that he died while baking
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with dark payoff. The casual delivery of tragic news works well.

jokes.text: Mutiny? On the bounty? What have you been smoking? Opium. Besides that?
jokes.setup: Captain dismisses mutiny threat
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of opium use when asked about smoking
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke. The casual opium admission is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Because we have no way of communicating with the outside world.
jokes.setup: What happens in Tahiti stays in Tahiti
jokes.punchline: Not by choice - they're literally isolated
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Vegas slogan. The literal explanation undercuts the sexy implication.

jokes.text: Tell me, has Robert Fulton invented the steamboat yet? Any day now.
jokes.setup: Asking about technological developments
jokes.punchline: Wildly optimistic timeline for steamboat invention
jokes.explanation: Mild historical humor about anachronistic expectations. Fits Wiggum's character confusion.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, I don't look like that. Make another. Yes, lord ugly-face.
jokes.setup: Burns complaining about his portrait
jokes.punchline: Artist agrees to redo it while calling him ugly-face
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with Burns' vanity. The insult delivery works well.

jokes.text: I order you to forget it.
jokes.setup: Crew wishing they were back in Tahiti
jokes.punchline: Impossible order to forget good memories
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Burns' authoritarianism. The impossibility of the order is the joke.

jokes.text: Rounder! I hear edges!
jokes.setup: Demanding crew sing in a round
jokes.punchline: Impossible critique of vocal performance geometry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist critique applying geometric concepts to singing. Perfectly captures Burns' impossible standards.

jokes.text: You don't sound like you've ever been on a boat in your life.
jokes.setup: Criticizing crew's singing of 'Row, Row, Row Your Boat'
jokes.punchline: Absurd critique since they're literally on a boat
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation. The absurdity of critiquing boat experience while on a boat works well.

jokes.text: You made it from the head of the last guy who had a suggestion.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions suggestion box
jokes.punchline: Made from previous suggester's severed head
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark visual gag. The casual delivery of horrific information is perfect.

jokes.text: Row, row, merrily, merrily, bound the down gently down the... merrily down the... I cannot do it! I'm only one man.
jokes.setup: Forced to sing round while rowing alone
jokes.punchline: Breaks down from impossibility of singing harmony solo
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the impossibility of the task. Willie's breakdown is well-earned.

jokes.text: Swim faster or I'll have you made into soup.
jokes.setup: Bart now captain threatening Burns in water
jokes.punchline: Casual threat of cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about maritime survival. Fits the reversal of power dynamic well.

jokes.text: You call this deep?
jokes.setup: Burns diving underwater to escape
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude even while drowning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - maintaining superiority even in defeat. Great economy.

jokes.text: Those are penguins.
jokes.setup: Bart promises most beautiful women in world
jokes.punchline: What he's seeing are actually penguins
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff. The visual gag of mistaking penguins for women works well.

jokes.text: Oh, boy, do I suck? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Bart realizing his navigation failures
jokes.punchline: Immediate honest confirmation of his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment. The blunt honesty works for the character reversal.

jokes.text: Disco fever! The only cure is to do a little dance, make a little love... get down tonight. Wait a minute. What was the second one?
jokes.setup: Disco Stu announcing outbreak and cure
jokes.punchline: Belatedly realizes he mentioned 'make a little love'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with delayed realization. Fits Disco Stu's oblivious personality.

jokes.text: Ten-four, good buddy. I'm Chevy Chase and you're not. Willy Stargell!
jokes.setup: Captain Burns greeting in 1970s style
jokes.punchline: Random string of 70s cultural references
jokes.explanation: Mild 70s nostalgia humor. The random references feel dated and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Your mission today involves going undercover at a wet t-shirt contest. Just get you wet... maybe not so lovable.
jokes.setup: Parody of Charlie's Angels briefing
jokes.punchline: Homer's sexual interest makes him less lovable
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer sexual response. The correction from narrator is mildly amusing but not special.

jokes.text: Our son shlomo is working on a kibbutz in haifa. We're schlepping him some kreplach. We're jewish, all right!
jokes.setup: Jewish couple explaining their trip
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top use of Jewish terminology and proud declaration
jokes.explanation: Heavy-handed ethnic humor relying on stereotypical language. Not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I think we're heading for disaster and most of you will not be saved unless the captain is attentive we'll all be crushed by a huge wave...
jokes.setup: Band singing on cruise ship
jokes.punchline: Song lyrics predict the actual disaster about to happen
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke with the band literally singing the plot. Good prophetic irony.

jokes.text: My wig! I mean, my natural hair!
jokes.setup: Burns' hair flying off in disaster
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals it's a wig while trying to cover it up
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns vanity moment with good verbal slip. The cover-up attempt makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Mama mia! What a time to be carrying the soup.
jokes.setup: Ship disaster happening
jokes.punchline: Chef's complaint about timing focused on soup delivery
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about misplaced priorities during disaster. Moderately effective.

jokes.text: Cool! No gravity!
jokes.setup: Ship flipping upside down
jokes.punchline: Bart's excitement about experiencing weightlessness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's ability to find fun in disaster. Appropriately childlike.

jokes.text: Goin' out with a sight gag. Nice.
jokes.setup: Krusty presumably dying in disaster
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about the comedic nature of his death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on comedy structure. Perfect for Krusty's character as a professional comedian.

jokes.text: I got an idea: Climb into my mouth.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to help family in disaster
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical survival solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely absurd but delivered with confidence. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Lord, with you watching over me, I shall feel no pain. AAAHHHHH!
jokes.setup: Ned expressing faith that God will protect him
jokes.punchline: Immediately screams in pain
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic reversal with perfect timing. Good character work showing Ned's faith being tested.

jokes.text: Homer, the ship's upside down and hundreds of people are dead! ...Three... two... one... Happy new year!
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out the disaster during countdown
jokes.punchline: Homer continues New Year's countdown despite catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - completely oblivious to disaster while focused on celebration. Dark but character-perfect.

jokes.text: 'Aw's are cheap. I'm lookin' for some action.
jokes.setup: People responding sympathetically to her loneliness
jokes.punchline: Selma dismisses sympathy, wants romance
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on audience manipulation combined with character moment. Sharp writing.

jokes.text: So, now which way do we go in this farkaktah-flippity ship?
jokes.setup: Asking for directions in upside-down ship
jokes.punchline: Yiddish curse word combined with made-up 'flippity'
jokes.explanation: Mild ethnic humor with creative wordplay. The 'flippity' addition works moderately well.

jokes.text: What? I have great ass suction.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be in bathroom during crisis
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate overshare about bathroom abilities
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer TMI moment. Character-appropriate but relies on crude humor.

jokes.text: I'll use my swiss army bone.
jokes.setup: Need tool to open jammed hatchway
jokes.punchline: Dog's bone described as multi-tool
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining Swiss Army knife with dog bone. Good visual gag concept.

jokes.text: I'm alone again... naturally
jokes.setup: Moe volunteering for heroic rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Sings depressing song as 'inspirational' music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - even his heroic inspiration is depressing. Great character integration with song choice.

jokes.text: Don't tell me what to do.
jokes.setup: Dying Moe asking Homer to tell world he saved everyone
jokes.punchline: Homer's reflexive defiance even to dying request
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - even Moe's dying wish triggers his contrarian nature. Dark but brilliant.

jokes.text: I don't know which pains me more: The flame or the irony! Can you believe it's the irony?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy caught in fire
jokes.punchline: Intellectual analysis of his own suffering
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor perfectly suited to Comic Book Guy's character. The intellectual analysis of physical/emotional pain is inspired.

jokes.text: At last I'm not gay for skeleton Kearney. Our ribs got tangled 75 years ago. That's not gay. Right. You keep believing that.
jokes.setup: Skeletons finally separating after decades
jokes.punchline: Awkward conversation about their forced intimacy
jokes.explanation: Weird dark humor about skeleton intimacy. The uncomfortable conversation continues their bullying dynamic from life.

tmdb_overview: As the family waits for their food at “The Frying Dutchman,” they pass the time by telling stories. Lisa tells about the Mayflower’s journey to America, where Marge and her kids are Pilgrims and they are joined by a knave named Homer who joins their family to escape the authorities. Bart recounts the story of Mutiny on the Bounty featuring Skinner as Captain Bligh and Bart as Mr. Christian. Homer tells the tale of the maiden voyage of a cruise ship in the 1970s featuring of a ship that can’t flip over, which of course it does and the attempts of the survivors to get out of the ship alive.
